info.name: 🏥 FlowCare API — Complete Test Suite
info.description:
# FlowCare Queue & Appointment Booking System

Complete API test collection covering all endpoints with role-based authentication, automated variable capture, and response validation.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start
1. Set `baseUrl` collection variable (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Run **🔐 Authentication → Register Customer** (select an image for `idImage`)
3. Run **🌐 Public → List Available Slots** — auto-captures `slotId` & `slotId2`
4. Run requests in folder order for full coverage

---

## 🔑 Default Credentials (from seed)
| Role | Email | Password |
|---|---|---|
| Admin | admin@flowcare.com | Admin@1234 |
| Manager (Muscat) | manager.muscat@flowcare.com | Manager@1234 |
| Manager (Salalah) | manager.salalah@flowcare.com | Manager@1234 |
| Staff (Muscat) | dr.sara@flowcare.com | Staff@1234 |
| Staff (Salalah) | dr.mona@flowcare.com | Staff@1234 |

---

## 📦 Auto-Captured Variables
| Variable | Captured By |
|---|---|
| `customerId` | Register Customer |
| `slotId` | List Available Slots (index 0) |
| `slotId2` | List Available Slots (index 1) |
| `appointmentId` | Book Appointment |
| `createdSlotId` | Create Slot |

---

## 📁 Folder Structure
1. 🏥 Health Check
2. 🔐 Authentication
3. 🌐 Public Endpoints
4. 👤 Customer — My Appointments
5. 👨‍⚕️ Staff & Manager — Appointments
6. 🕐 Slot Management
7. 👥 Staff Management
8. 🧍 Customer Management
9. 📊 Audit Logs
10. ⚙️ System Configuration

item.name: 🏥  Health Check
item.description: Verify the API server is alive and responding.

request.description: Returns server status and timestamp. No authentication required.

item.name: 🔐  Authentication
item.description: Customer registration and login for all roles.

formdata.description: ⚠️ SELECT an image file (.jpg/.png) — max 5MB — REQUIRED

request.description:
Register a new customer account with an ID image.

⚠️ **Action Required:** Click the `idImage` field and select an image file before sending.

On success, `customerId` is automatically saved to collection variables.

exec: // Always generate a fresh email before registering

request.description: Verify Admin login. Returns user profile with `admin` role.
Verify Branch Manager login. Returns `branch_manager` role and `branchId`.
Verify Staff login. Returns `staff` role, `staffId`, and `branchId`.
Verify Customer login. Run **Register Customer** first.
Negative test — expects 401 Unauthorized with wrong credentials.

item.name: 🌐  Public Endpoints
item.description: No authentication required. Browse branches, services, and available slots.

query.description: Search by branch name or location

request.description: List all branches. Supports pagination (`page`, `size`) and text search (`term`).
Search branches by name or location using the `term` query parameter.

exec: const match = names.some(n => n.includes('muscat')) || locations.some(l => l.includes('muscat'));

request.description: Get a single branch with its associated service types.
Negative test — expects 404 for a non-existent branch ID.
List all service types offered by a specific branch.

List available (unbooked, not soft-deleted) slots for a branch.

Auto-captures `slotId` and `slotId2` for use in booking and rescheduling requests.

exec: pm.test('At least 2 slots available (needed for reschedule test)', () => {

item.name: List Available Slots — Filter by Service & Date

request.description: List available slots filtered by service type (and optionally a date).

exec: pm.test('All results match service type filter', () => {

item.name: 👤  Customer — My Appointments
item.description:
Authenticated customer operations: book, view, reschedule, and cancel appointments.

⚠️ **Prerequisites:**
- Run **Register Customer** first
- Run **List Available Slots** to populate `slotId` and `slotId2`

request.description:
Book an appointment for a slot.

**Auto-captures** `appointmentId` on success.

Optionally attach a file (image/PDF).
Negative test — attempt to book an already-booked slot. Expects 409 Conflict.
List all appointments for the authenticated customer.
Get full details of a specific appointment including slot, branch, and service type.

Move the appointment to a different available slot.

Uses `slotId2` (captured from List Available Slots at index 1). The original slot (`slotId`) is freed after rescheduling.
Cancel an active appointment. The slot is freed and made available again.
Negative test — staff cannot access the customer-only `/my` endpoint. Expects 403.

item.name: 👨‍⚕️  Staff & Manager — Appointments
item.description: View and manage appointments (role-filtered). Staff see assigned appointments only. Managers see branch-only. Admins see all.

formdata.description: slotId is available again after the customer cancelled their appointment

request.description: Books a FRESH appointment for status update testing. The original appointmentId was cancelled in the Customer folder, so we need a new active appointment. slotId is available again after cancellation.

query.description: Filter by status: PENDING | CONFIRMED | CHECKED_IN | COMPLETED | NO_SHOW | CANCELLED
Search by customer name, email, branch, or service

request.description: Admin can see appointments from ALL branches.
Branch Manager sees only appointments from their assigned branch (branch-scoped).

exec: pm.test('Manager sees only their branch appointments', () => {

request.description: Staff can only see appointments that are assigned to them.
Update appointment to CHECKED_IN. Valid statuses: `CONFIRMED`, `CHECKED_IN`, `COMPLETED`, `NO_SHOW`.
Mark appointment as COMPLETED by staff.
Negative test — invalid status value. Expects 400 Bad Request.

Download the attachment file for an appointment.

Accessible by: staff, manager, admin, or the appointment's own customer.

Returns `404` if no attachment was uploaded.
Get the queue position for a specific appointment. Returns the position in the branch queue (PENDING/CONFIRMED appointments ahead of this one + 1).

exec: // queuePosition is a number or null depending on appointment status

item.name: 🕐  Slot Management — Manager / Admin
item.description:
Create, update, soft-delete, and cleanup appointment slots.

**Branch Managers** can only manage slots in their assigned branch.
**Admins** can manage slots across all branches.

request.description: Create a single time slot for a branch. `serviceTypeId` must be offered by the branch. **Auto-captures** `createdSlotId`.

exec: // Auto-capture created slot ID for update/delete tests

request.description: Create multiple slots at once by sending a JSON array. Returns an array of created slots.
Negative test — Muscat manager tries to create a slot in Salalah branch. Expects 403.
Update a slot's time or availability. Uses `createdSlotId` captured from Create Slot.
Soft-delete a slot (sets `deletedAt` timestamp). The slot is hidden from public listings but the record is preserved. An `AuditLog` entry is created.
Admin-only view that includes soft-deleted slots. Non-admins always see only active available slots.

Manually trigger hard-deletion of soft-deleted slots that have exceeded the retention period.

This is also run automatically by the daily cron job at midnight.

**Admin only.**
Negative test — only admin can trigger cleanup. Manager gets 403.

item.name: 👥  Staff Management — Manager / Admin
item.description:
View and manage staff assignments to services.

**Branch Managers** can only manage staff within their branch.
**Admins** have system-wide access.

query.description: Search by name, email, or branch

request.description: Admin sees staff from all branches including managers.

exec: pm.expect(json.total).to.be.at.least(6, 'Expected at least 6 seeded staff members');

request.description: Branch Manager sees only staff in their branch (branch-scoped).

exec: pm.test('Manager sees only their branch staff', () => {

request.description: Assign a staff member to a service type. The service type must be offered by the staff's branch. Idempotent — re-assigning does not create duplicates.
Remove a staff member's assignment from a service type. Creates an audit log entry.
Create a new staff member. Branch managers can only create staff for their own branch. Only admins can set isManager: true.

item.name: Assign Staff to Branch — Admin Only

request.description: Admin-only endpoint to reassign a staff member to a different branch.

item.name: 🧍  Customer Management — Manager / Admin
item.description:
View customer records and ID images.

**Managers** can list and view customers (no ID image access).
**Admins** have full access including customer ID image download.

query.description: Search by name, email, or phone

request.description: List all registered customers with paginated results and optional search.

exec: // Capture a customerId if not already set

request.description: Get a customer's full profile including ID image path. Uses `customerId` captured during registration.

item.name: Download Customer ID Image — Admin Only

request.description: Download the customer's ID image file. Returns the image with correct `Content-Type`. Admin access only.
Negative test — only admins can access ID images. Manager gets 403.

item.name: 📊  Audit Logs
item.description:
View and export the audit trail of sensitive actions.

**Branch Managers** see logs for their branch only.
**Admins** see all logs and can export as CSV.

query.description: Filter by action, role, or target type

request.description: Branch manager sees only audit log entries for their branch.

exec: pm.test('All logs belong to manager branch', () => {

request.description: Admin sees all audit log entries across all branches.

exec: pm.test('All audit logs returned for admin', () => {

item.name: Export Audit Logs as CSV — Admin Only

request.description:
Download all audit logs as a CSV file.

💡 **Tip:** In Postman, click **Send and Download** to save the CSV file to disk.
Negative test — staff cannot access audit logs. Expects 403.

item.name: ⚙️  System Configuration — Admin Only
item.description:
Configure system-wide settings and monitor queue status.

All configuration endpoints require **Admin** role.

request.description: Get the current soft-delete retention period in days. Default: 30 days.
Update the soft-delete retention period. Soft-deleted slots older than this many days will be hard-deleted by the cleanup job.
Negative test — negative days value should return 400 Bad Request.
Restore the retention period to the default 30 days after testing.
Get the real-time active queue count for a branch (appointments with PENDING or CONFIRMED status).
Get the active queue count for Salalah branch.
Negative test — accessing config without authentication. Expects 401.
